# Song Leader Training

A cappella song-leading practice tool with real-time pitch detection, hymn melody playback, and a YIN-algorithm grading system. Client-side only React app, no backend, no auth.

> End-of-session protocol: see bottom of this file.

## Active Work
_None — stable practice tool. Continue extracting from App.jsx and pruning legacy root files when touching related functionality._

<!-- SCAN:AUTO:START — Generated by security-scan-prompt v7.2. Do not edit this section manually. -->

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Tech |
|---|---|
| Frontend | React 18.3.1, Vite 6, Canvas API, Web Audio API + AudioWorklet, VexFlow 5 (notation), @tonejs/midi 2 (MIDI parsing) |
| Backend | None (client-side only) |
| Data | Static JSON in `public/` (`hymn_index.json`, `hymn_melodies/`); no database, no localStorage |
| Auth | None |
| Hosting | Vercel (`vercel.json` headers block) |
| Testing | None |
| Integrations | Google Fonts (DM Sans + DM Serif Display via CSS @import), `soundfont-player` fetching `church_organ` from `gleitz.github.io` (MusyngKite CDN), `getUserMedia` for microphone |

## Architecture

### Folder Structure
```
Song-Leader-Training/
├── index.html                       # Vite entry, PWA meta tags
├── src/
│   ├── App.jsx                      # Main component (1,430 lines)
│   ├── main.jsx                     # ReactDOM mount
│   ├── audio/
│   │   ├── pitch-engine.js          # Main-thread pitch controller (610 lines)
│   │   ├── recorder.js              # AudioRecorder class (380 lines)
│   │   ├── grader.js                # Performance scoring (404 lines)
│   │   ├── midi-parser.js           # @tonejs/midi wrapper (204 lines)
│   │   └── index.js                 # Barrel re-exports
│   └── components/
│       ├── PitchVisualizer.jsx      # Canvas-based pitch display (360 lines)
│       └── NotationDisplay.jsx      # VexFlow notation rendering (363 lines)
├── public/
│   ├── pitch-processor.js           # AudioWorklet (audio thread, YIN algorithm, 350 lines)
│   ├── hymn_index.json              # Hymn metadata
│   ├── hymn_melodies/               # Per-hymn JSON melody data
│   ├── sheet_music/                 # PNG sheet-music images
│   ├── manifest.json                # PWA manifest
│   ├── robots.txt
│   └── .well-known/security.txt
├── zions-hymns-trainer.jsx          # LEGACY DEAD CODE (566 lines, root)
├── zions-hymns-trainer_3.jsx        # LEGACY DEAD CODE (867 lines, root)
├── eslint.config.js, vite.config.js, vercel.json, .nvmrc (Node 22)
└── package.json, package-lock.json
```

### Key Files
- `src/App.jsx` (1,430 lines) — entire UI, meter engine, melody generator, hymn loader, practice/grading flow, inline CSS (`const css = ...`).
- `src/audio/pitch-engine.js` (610 lines) — main-thread controller that wraps the AudioWorklet, applies smoothing (outlier rejection → median filter → EMA).
- `public/pitch-processor.js` (350 lines) — AudioWorklet running YIN with parabolic interpolation on a dedicated audio thread.
- `src/audio/grader.js` (404 lines) — pitch accuracy / rhythm / stability scoring.
- `src/audio/recorder.js` (380 lines) — AudioRecorder class capturing pitch history during practice.
- `src/components/NotationDisplay.jsx` (363 lines) — VexFlow renderer.
- `src/components/PitchVisualizer.jsx` (360 lines) — Canvas pitch display, bypasses React for 60fps.
- `vercel.json` — full security header block (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy).

### Data Flow
Mic → `getUserMedia` → `AudioContext` → `pitch-processor.js` AudioWorklet runs YIN on each buffer → posts pitch frames back to `pitch-engine.js` → smoothing pipeline → `PitchVisualizer.jsx` (Canvas, no React re-render). Hymn data: `public/hymn_index.json` → fetch in `App.jsx` → on selection, fetch `public/hymn_melodies/<id>.json` and optional MIDI → `midi-parser.js` decodes → `NotationDisplay.jsx` renders staff via VexFlow → `grader.js` compares detected pitches against reference frames.

### External API Calls
- `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans...&family=DM+Serif+Display` — CSS `@import` from inline stylesheet in `App.jsx` line 259, page-load.
- `https://fonts.gstatic.com/...` — font file fetches triggered by the CSS @import.
- `https://gleitz.github.io/midi-js-soundfonts/MusyngKite/...` — `soundfont-player` loads the `church_organ` instrument when the user starts melody playback.
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/...` — fallback CDN allowed by CSP `connect-src` for soundfont assets.

## Environment Variables

None — fully static client-side app. No API keys, no backend secrets, no `.env` file (confirmed: no `import.meta.env` / `process.env` references in source).

## Security Notes

### Active Flags

| Severity | Category | Confidence | Description |
|----------|----------|------------|-------------|
| P4 | npm-cve-dev-only | 1.0 | `npm audit` reports 3 vulnerabilities (vite ≤6.4.2 high; js-yaml 4.0.0–4.1.1 moderate; @babel/core ≤7.29.0 low) — all in dev/build tooling, none shipped to the client bundle. vite advisories are dev-server-only (Windows `server.fs.deny` bypass GHSA-fx2h-pf6j-xcff, NTLMv2 leak via launch-editor GHSA-v6wh-96g9-6wx3). Run `npm audit fix` on the next touch; no production exposure. |
| P3 | cdn-no-sri | 0.85 | `App.jsx:259` imports Google Fonts via CSS `@import` with no SRI hash; `soundfont-player` fetches `church_organ` from `gleitz.github.io` (MusyngKite CDN) with no integrity check. |
| P3 | deprecated-packages | 0.85 | `soundfont-player@0.12.0` last published 2018 — unmaintained, but no maintained drop-in replacement exists. Pin/self-host before any CDN swap. |

### Watch List (confidence < 0.8)
- **xss-innerhtml** (0.35) — `src/components/NotationDisplay.jsx:306` concatenates a caught exception's `e.message` into `container.innerHTML` in the VexFlow render fallback. Guardrail #4 (no `innerHTML` with dynamic data). Exploitability is very low: the message originates from VexFlow reacting to locally-generated melody data and same-origin hymn JSON in `public/` — there is no LLM output, no user free-text, and no cross-user data path. Still worth converting to `textContent` (build the wrapper element, set its text) the next time the file is touched.

### Accepted Risks
- **No authentication** — intentional, public hymn-singing practice tool. No user data, no accounts.
- **Public GitHub repo** — `taylorzeltwanger91/Song-Leader-Training` is public. No secrets in the codebase, so the public flag is safe.
- **`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on static assets** — no API routes, no auth, nothing cross-origin to protect.
- **`script-src 'unsafe-eval'` in CSP** — required by `soundfont-player` and `@tonejs/midi`. Acceptable for a static practice tool with no user-supplied input.
- **Empty/graceful catch blocks in audio paths** — `App.jsx` (oscillator playback, recorder stop/destroy) and audio modules swallow errors to degrade gracefully (return null, reset playing state, continue cleanup). These wrap optional Web Audio APIs (feature-detection pattern); no auth, payment, webhook, or data-write path is involved, so they are not flagged as `swallowed-exception`.
- **Console statements in audio modules** — `recorder.js`, `pitch-engine.js`, `midi-parser.js` use `console.warn/error` for legitimate audio debugging. The lone `console.log` in `midi-parser.js:175` logs MIDI parse metadata (title, track/note counts) — no sensitive data; benign diagnostic, not flagged.

### Resolved
- **file-over-500** (2026-06-11) — under v6.8's uniform 1500-line threshold, no file qualifies. `src/App.jsx` (1,430) and `pitch-engine.js` (610) previously flagged under the old 500-line guidance now clear. Cohesion-based "consider splitting" guidance retained in Project-Specific.
- **console-log-prod** (2026-06-11) — `midi-parser.js:175` logs no sensitive data; `debug-logging` (P4) requires sensitive data. Dropped from active flags.
- **npm-cve-high** (2026-05-06) — Previous P1 covering picomatch ReDoS / rollup / vite path traversal in dev deps cleared by upgrade. NOTE (2026-06-30): 3 NEW dev-only advisories have since surfaced (vite/js-yaml/@babel/core) — see the active P4 `npm-cve-dev-only` flag. None ship to the client bundle; the earlier "0 vulnerabilities" state no longer holds.
- **missing-csp** (2026-05-02) — full CSP header added to `vercel.json`, confirmed served by production with correct directives.
- **missing-x-frame-options** (2026-05-02) — `X-Frame-Options: DENY` confirmed deployed.
- **missing-x-content-type** (2026-05-02) — `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` confirmed deployed.
- **missing-referrer-policy** (2026-05-02) — `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin` confirmed deployed.
- **missing-hsts** (2026-05-02) — `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` confirmed deployed.
- **missing-permissions-policy** (2026-05-02) — `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), geolocation=(), interest-cohort=()` confirmed deployed.
- **missing-security-txt** (2026-05-02) — `public/.well-known/security.txt` added with mailto contact, 2027-12-31 expiry, en preferred-language. Verified served from production (HTTP 200 this scan).
- **missing-robots-txt** (2026-05-02) — `public/robots.txt` (`User-agent: * / Allow: /`) verified served from production (HTTP 200 this scan).
- **debug-console-log** (2026-05-02) — 24 debug `console.log` statements removed from audio modules.
- **node-eol** (2026-05-02) — `.nvmrc` bumped 20 → 22 ahead of Node 20 EOL; Node 22 is current LTS.
- **source-maps-in-prod** — Vite production builds do not emit `.map` files by default; verified again in `dist/` this scan (no `.map`, no `.env` in build output).

## Guardrails

### Universal (apply to all projects)

1. Never hardcode API keys or secrets — use `.env` + `.env.example` from first commit
2. Every API route needs CORS origin validation, input validation, and auth
3. Write RLS/Firestore rules the same day you create a table
4. Never use `innerHTML` with dynamic data — use `textContent` or framework escaping
5. API proxies must restrict destination URLs with an allowlist (SSRF prevention)
6. Auth cookies must contain signed claims (HMAC), not plain booleans
7. If a secret was ever committed to a public repo, rotate it immediately
8. No `console.log` with sensitive data in production
9. Run `npx prettier --write .` before committing — keep a `.prettierrc` in the repo root

### Project-Specific
- **Do not add code to `src/App.jsx`.** It is already 1,430 lines. New features go in new files under `src/components/` or `src/audio/`. When fixing a bug there, take the opportunity to extract the surrounding section.
- **Delete legacy root `zions-hymns-trainer*.jsx` files** when touching related functionality — they are not imported and only confuse search.
- **Do not introduce auth, env vars, or backend services.** This is a local practice tool by design. If a feature requires server state, raise it before implementing.
- **Pin or self-host the soundfont before swapping CDNs.** `soundfont-player` is unmaintained; if it ever breaks, the migration path is a maintained alternative + bundled `church_organ` asset, not a fresh remote CDN.
- **Keep CSP intact in `vercel.json`.** Any new external host (analytics, fonts, audio source) needs an explicit `connect-src`/`style-src`/`font-src` entry — do not weaken `default-src 'self'`.
- **Replace `innerHTML` with `textContent` in `src/components/NotationDisplay.jsx:306`.** The render-error fallback concatenates `e.message` into `innerHTML`; build a `<div>` and set `.textContent` instead. Low risk today but a Guardrail #4 violation.

## Deployed Surface
- **URL:** https://song-leader-training.vercel.app (Vercel default — no custom domain)
- **CSP:** `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data:; img-src 'self' data: blob:; connect-src 'self' https://gleitz.github.io https://raw.githubusercontent.com; media-src 'self' blob:; worker-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'` — confirmed served.
- **HSTS:** `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` — confirmed served.
- **X-Frame-Options:** `DENY` — confirmed served.
- **X-Content-Type-Options:** `nosniff` — confirmed served.
- **Referrer-Policy:** `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` — confirmed served.
- **Permissions-Policy:** `camera=(), geolocation=(), interest-cohort=()` — confirmed served.
- **Server header:** `Vercel` (platform disclosed — cannot be removed via `vercel.json`).
- **CORS:** `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on static assets (acceptable — no API routes, no auth).
- **`/.well-known/security.txt`:** present (HTTP 200).
- **`/robots.txt`:** present (HTTP 200).
- **Source maps:** none in production build.
- **DNS audit:** skipped — no custom domain (Vercel default).

## Strengths

Full security-header set (CSP with locked-down `default-src 'self'`, HSTS, X-Frame DENY, nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) verified live in production, no secrets/env/backend attack surface (fully static client-side app), the only npm advisories confined to non-shipping dev tooling, ESLint `no-undef` enforced via a `prebuild` lint gate, and YIN pitch detection correctly isolated to an AudioWorklet thread for performance.

## Dev Commands

```bash
npm run dev      # vite — local dev server
npm run lint     # eslint .
npm run prebuild # npm run lint (runs automatically before build)
npm run build    # vite build (lint gate runs first)
npm run preview  # vite preview — serve the production build locally
```

## Scope & Boundaries
Local-only, client-side practice tool for a cappella song leaders. No accounts, no server state, no analytics, no payments. Hymn data is checked into `public/`. Microphone is requested at runtime for pitch detection; nothing leaves the device.

Out of scope: multi-user features, cloud sync, recordings being uploaded anywhere, AI grading, monetization.

## Conventions
- Single-file `App.jsx` is intentional — extract only when adding a new feature or when the file forces a search miss.
- Inline CSS as a `const css = ...` template literal injected once is the established pattern. Don't introduce a CSS framework.
- Audio modules use `console.warn`/`console.error` for diagnostics; `console.log` is reserved for one-off MIDI parse summaries (and even those should be cleaned up).
- React component files are PascalCase; audio modules are kebab-case.
- VexFlow and Canvas paths bypass React rendering on purpose for 60 fps; do not "fix" by lifting state.

## Key Patterns
- **YIN on AudioWorklet:** pitch detection runs on the audio thread (`public/pitch-processor.js`), main thread only handles smoothing and rendering. This is a hard performance ceiling — keep it.
- **Smoothing pipeline:** outlier rejection → median filter → EMA. Tweaks should be unit-tested with recorded fixtures, not eyeballed.
- **Grading:** `grader.js` consumes a reference melody (frames with start/end/midi) and a detected pitch trace, returns pitch / rhythm / stability scores 0-100.
- **Hymn loading:** lazy fetch on selection. `hymn_index.json` is the only file always loaded.

## Common Issues
- **`unsafe-eval` in CSP** is required by `soundfont-player` (uses `Function` constructor). Removing it breaks playback in all major browsers.
- **iOS Safari** requires a user gesture to resume `AudioContext`. The mic-start button is the gesture; do not auto-start.
- **MusyngKite CDN** occasionally 404s — `connect-src` already allows the GitHub-raw fallback so soundfont-player can fail over.
- **VexFlow 5** changed several factory APIs from VexFlow 4 — pin and read changelogs before bumping.

## Session Maintenance Protocol

At the end of every substantive session, propose targeted edits to CLAUDE.md or `.claude/rules/*.md`. Domain-specific detail belongs in the rule file, not here. The SCAN:AUTO block above is auto-managed — never hand-edit; the next scan will overwrite.
